assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 45-54 explicitly reference apply_filter: "Apply license filter using `apply_filter`: When `license` is `Some`, use the `apply_filter` function from `common/src/db/query.rs` to parse the comma-separated values and generate the SQL `IN` clause." Code example shows `use common::db::query::apply_filter;` and direct call `query = apply_filter(query, "package_license", "license", license_param)?;`. The "Files NOT Modified" section (line 115) confirms: "common/src/db/query.rs -- The `apply_filter` function already supports comma-separated multi-value parsing; reused directly, no modifications needed."

assertion_results.text: The plan references following the filter pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs as a structural guide for implementing the license filter
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 15 states: "Following the pattern in `modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs` where the advisory list endpoint has an optional `severity` field in its `Query` struct, add an `Option<String>` field named `license`." Line 25 states: "This mirrors how the advisory endpoint passes its `severity` parameter to `AdvisoryService::list`." Line 27 references: "Use the same error-handling pattern as the advisory endpoint." Line 35: "matching the advisory service pattern where `AdvisoryService::list` accepts an optional severity filter parameter."

assertion_results.text: The plan references using entity/src/package_license.rs for the JOIN query rather than writing raw SQL or creating a new entity
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 56-67 describe: "Join with `package_license` entity: Add a JOIN to the `package_license` table (defined in `entity/src/package_license.rs`) so the filter can match against the license column. Use SeaORM's join API." Code example shows `use entity::package_license;` and SeaORM join call. The "Files NOT Modified" section (line 114) confirms: "entity/src/package_license.rs -- Already exists with the package-license mapping; used as-is for the JOIN, no modifications needed."

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: reuse-analysis.md covers all three candidates with dedicated sections: (1) "Reuse Candidate 1: `common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter`" (lines 6-31) explains it is called directly to split comma-separated values and generate SQL IN clauses; (2) "Reuse Candidate 2: `modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs`" (lines 33-63) explains it is used as a structural template with a mapping table showing advisory-to-package correspondence; (3) "Reuse Candidate 3: `entity/src/package_license.rs`" (lines 65-84) explains the entity is used for SeaORM JOIN. Each section includes how the candidate is reused and what is NOT duplicated.
